title: Ordered-categorical CatBoost plus AFT duration blend
summary: CatBoost’s ordered CTR machinery is designed to compute categorical statistics from previous objects only, which is attractive for high-cardinality `Driver/Race/Compound/Stint` interactions; scikit-learn’s docs explicitly warn that non-cross-fitted target encoding can catastrophically overfit.
rationale: CatBoost’s ordered CTR machinery is designed to compute categorical statistics from previous objects only, which is attractive for high-cardinality `Driver/Race/Compound/Stint` interactions; scikit-learn’s docs explicitly warn that non-cross-fitted target encoding can catastrophically overfit. Separately, XGBoost’s AFT objective uses censored time-to-event information that a plain binary next-lap classifier discards.
implementation_hint: Train one CatBoost specialist on raw categorical interactions plus causal numeric features, using race-aware folds and stable within-race ordering. Train a second model as AFT survival on `laps_until_next_pit` with censoring at race end, convert its survival curve to next-lap pit probability, then blend the CatBoost/current-model/AFT outputs in rank space using fold-wise weights selected on grouped CV.
expected_effect: Diversifies error modes, improves handling of rare categorical interactions, and may transfer better to public LB than a single binary classifier.
risk: Most complex idea here: AFT target construction, CatBoost ordering, and blend-weight selection can all leak if they are not fully out-of-fold by grouped race splits.
